ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for cost snapshot: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover cost snapshot, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the disaster-recovery pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for cost snapshot.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for cost snapshot.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Evidence Store and Time Machine cost snapshot inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: cost-snapshot_disaster-recovery_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for cost snapshot disaster-recovery that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for cost snapshot; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for cost snapshot.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for cost snapshot recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for cost snapshot.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for cost snapshot.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing cost snapshot data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling cost snapshot inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for cost snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: Given cost snapshot data exists, when the disaster-recovery inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: cost snapshot connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for investigation replay: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for investigation replay operational-excellence posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for investigation replay.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for investigation replay.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Evidence Store and Time Machine investigation replay automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: investigation-replay_operational-excellence_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for investigation replay operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for investigation replay; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for investigation replay.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for investigation replay recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for investigation replay.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for investigation replay.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing investigation replay data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling investigation replay automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for investigation replay.
acceptance_criteria: Given investigation replay data exists, when the operational-excellence automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: investigation replay connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for immutability policy: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for immutability policy with scalability guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for immutability policy.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for immutability policy.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Evidence Store and Time Machine immutability policy operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: immutability-policy_scalability_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for immutability policy scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for immutability policy; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for immutability policy.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for immutability policy recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for immutability policy.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for immutability policy.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing immutability policy data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling immutability policy operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for immutability policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given immutability policy data exists, when the scalability operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: immutability policy connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
